commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And the glory of the LORD went up from the midst of the city, and stood upon the mountain which is on the east side of the city. Mount Olivet, whence he might behold the conflagration of the city, before his ascent into heaven. (Menochius) He leaves his habitation by degrees, to show how Jerusalem would be treated, and the Jews suffer after the ascension of our Saviour. Theodoret well observes, that the person on the throne represented Him, the upper part of the body being different from the lower, (chap. ) to denote the two natures.
